events.title: Brian May turns 79
events.blurb: Queen's legendary guitarist, songwriter, and astrophysicist Brian May was born on July 19, 1947.

angles.name: Brian May's Enduring Legacy
angles.copy: Seventy-nine years young today, the one and only Brian May continues to inspire. From iconic riffs to his work in astrophysics, he truly is a rock legend with universal appeal.

events.title: Mick Jagger turns 83
events.blurb: The charismatic frontman of The Rolling Stones, Sir Mick Jagger, was born on July 26, 1943.

angles.name: Mick Jagger's Ageless Rock
angles.copy: Eighty-three years old today and still rolling! Mick Jagger, the inimitable voice of The Rolling Stones, celebrates another year of rock and roll royalty.

events.title: MTV launched 45 years ago
events.blurb: On August 1, 1981, MTV launched with The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star' as its first broadcast music video, forever changing the music industry.

angles.name: MTV's Game-Changing Debut
angles.copy: Forty-five years ago today, a channel called MTV aired its first music video, 'Video Killed The Radio Star,' and the world of music was never the same. Remember the first time you saw a music video that blew you away?

events.title: Oasis's 'Definitely Maybe' turns 32
events.blurb: Oasis released their iconic debut album 'Definitely Maybe' on August 29, 1994, featuring hits like 'Supersonic' and 'Live Forever'.

angles.name: Definitely Maybe: Britpop Landmark
angles.copy: Thirty-two years since Oasis dropped 'Definitely Maybe' and cemented their place in music history. Still sounds as fresh and anthemic today. What's your favourite track?

events.title: Woodstock Festival's 57th anniversary
events.blurb: The legendary Woodstock Music & Art Fair, a pivotal event in music history, took place from August 15-18, 1969, in Bethel, New York.

angles.name: Woodstock: 3 Days of Peace & Music
angles.copy: Fifty-seven years ago, half a million people descended on a dairy farm for '3 Days of Peace & Music.' Woodstock defined a generation and its impact still resonates.

events.title: Anniversary of Elvis Presley's passing
events.blurb: Today marks 49 years since the death of the King of Rock and Roll, Elvis Presley, who passed away on August 16, 1977. He would have been 91.

angles.name: Remembering The King
angles.copy: Forty-nine years today, the music world lost an icon. Remembering Elvis Presley, whose voice and presence continue to captivate generations. The King would have been 91 this year.

events.title: Anniversary of Tupac Shakur being shot
events.blurb: On September 7, 1996, influential rapper Tupac Shakur was shot in a drive-by in Las Vegas; he later died on September 13. He would have been 55 this year.

angles.name: Tupac: A Voice Silenced Too Soon
angles.copy: Thirty years since the tragic shooting of Tupac Shakur. A pioneering voice in music, his impact remains profound. He would have been 55 this year.

events.title: Anniversary of Jimi Hendrix's passing
events.blurb: Guitar icon Jimi Hendrix died on September 18, 1970, at the age of 27. He would have been 84 this year.

angles.name: Jimi Hendrix: Guitar God Remembered
angles.copy: Fifty-six years since we lost the incomparable Jimi Hendrix. His revolutionary guitar playing continues to inspire. He would have been 84.

events.title: The Beatles' 'Abbey Road' turns 57
events.blurb: The Beatles released their iconic eleventh studio album 'Abbey Road' in the UK on September 26, 1969.

angles.name: Abbey Road: An Iconic Album
angles.copy: Fifty-seven years since The Beatles gave us 'Abbey Road'. That album cover, those tracks – pure genius. What's your go-to song from the record?

events.title: Chrissie Hynde turns 75
events.blurb: Chrissie Hynde, the formidable lead singer and songwriter of The Pretenders, was born on September 7, 1951.

angles.name: Chrissie Hynde: Rock & Roll Rebel
angles.copy: Happy 75th birthday to the incredible Chrissie Hynde! Her distinctive voice and fierce attitude have made The Pretenders a timeless force in rock.

events.title: Liam Gallagher turns 54
events.blurb: The outspoken frontman of Oasis and solo artist, Liam Gallagher, was born on September 21, 1972.

angles.name: Liam Gallagher: Still Rocking At 54
angles.copy: Our kid turns 54 today! Happy birthday to Liam Gallagher, the unmistakable voice of a generation. What's your favourite Liam moment?

events.title: John Lennon's 'Imagine' UK single anniversary
events.blurb: John Lennon's iconic peace anthem 'Imagine' was released as a single in the UK in October 1975, to promote the compilation album 'Shaved Fish'.

angles.name: Imagine: Lennon's Timeless Message
angles.copy: Fifty-one years since John Lennon's 'Imagine' first captivated the world. A powerful message of peace that still resonates today.

events.title: First iPod announced 25 years ago
events.blurb: Apple unveiled the original iPod on October 23, 2001, promising '1,000 songs in your pocket' and revolutionising portable music.

angles.name: The iPod: 1,000 Songs in Your Pocket
angles.copy: Twenty-five years ago, Apple announced the first iPod. Remember a time before streaming, when '1,000 songs in your pocket' was revolutionary?

events.title: James Bond Day
events.blurb: October 5th is celebrated annually as James Bond Day, marking the release date of the very first Bond film, 'Dr. No', in 1962.

angles.name: Shaken, Not Stirred: It's Bond Day
angles.copy: Grab your martini, it's James Bond Day! Celebrating 007 and over six decades of thrilling espionage and iconic soundtracks. Who's your favourite Bond?

events.title: John Lennon would have been 86
events.blurb: Today marks the birthday of The Beatles co-founder, singer, and songwriter John Lennon, who was born on October 9, 1940. He would have been 86.

angles.name: Remembering John Lennon
angles.copy: On what would have been his 86th birthday, we remember the visionary John Lennon. His music and message of peace continue to inspire.
